title: Warm up
content: Introductory slide showing three taxi driver perspectives from different Chinese cities: Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou. Each image depicts a driver in a car with distinct city backdrops and contexts, hinting at different themes for each city.
key_claims: Beijing scene shows a driver with tech-equipped car amid modern skyline and traditional architecture
Chongqing scene shows a driver reading a book with mountainous, layered city architecture in background
Hangzhou scene shows a driver using a smartphone app with AI/robot graphics and a lake/mountain backdrop

title: An Evolving Nation
content: Title slide introducing the presentation theme 'A Ride Through China', using taxi and iconic Chinese architectural imagery to set the stage for exploring China's development.
key_claims: Visual imagery includes a taxi sign and iconic Chinese buildings (Forbidden City-style architecture, illuminated pagoda-style buildings, and a tower)

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide introduces a structured framework for analyzing cities, moving from local features to development strategies and finally to outcomes and positive impact. An isometric cityscape illustration accompanies the process flow.
key_claims: Three-step process: Local Features → Development Strategies → Outcomes & Positive Impact
Framework is designed for investigating and researching urban development

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: An interactive matching exercise linking three Chinese cities (Beijing, Chongqing, Hangzhou) to their respective development strategies. Lines connect each city to a strategy, with the answers appearing crossed/mismatched in this rendering.
key_claims: Beijing is linked to 'Technology & tradition integration (融合)'
Chongqing is linked to 'Sustainable urban development'
Hangzhou is linked to 'Tourism & Economic growth'
Images show Forbidden City, illuminated cityscape, and a pagoda tower representing the three cities

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide reveals the correct pairing of each city with its development strategy, framed through taxi driver perspectives shown earlier. It concludes with a summary statement about China's evolving development approach tailored to local conditions.
key_claims: Beijing (highway/tech scene) corresponds to 'sustainable urban development'
Chongqing (mountain city scene) corresponds to 'tourism & economic growth'
Hangzhou (lake/AI scene) corresponds to 'technology & tradition integration'
Key message: 'An evolving nation and unique development strategies'
Key message: 'Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote city development' (因地制宜 - tailoring measures to local conditions)
